compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The registry is largely invalidated by the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or completely obscured. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable anchor due to the rabbit figurine, but the plant itself is severely diminished or absent.

inventory_reconciliation.p1_yellow_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.p2_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.p3_black_pot: Severe Decline: Only the rabbit anchor remains; plant foliage is absent.
inventory_reconciliation.p4_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.

plant_audit.p3: Rabbit figurine present; plant foliage appears to have undergone total senescence or removal.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and inert across all pots.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of crushed eggshells in the primary pot (P3/P4 area), likely an attempted soil amendment or pest deterrent.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows a single green stem remnant in the foreground pot.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_current: Progressive loss of the remaining green stem; the biome has transitioned to a state of total vegetative failure.

visual_health_inference.reasoning: The absence of chlorophyll-bearing tissue and the lack of structural integrity in the stems indicate that the specimens are no longer viable.

anomalies: Crushed eggshell fragments on soil surface (Intervention).
Total absence of expected botanical specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the indoor botanical biome. Starting from the earliest image, which showed a single, struggling stem, the sequence documents a complete decline. By the current state, all pots are devoid of living plant matter. The presence of eggshells suggests a failed attempt at intervention. The environment is currently non-productive.
